threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check the tube app obsessively, not realizing locals just use the bus or walk. It’s a whole different vibe when you stop planning every trip.
Last night on the 35, a guy in a bowler hat sang the whole way to Clapham. No one joined in, but we were all smirking. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Watching the 104 bus driver give free rides to a group of kids on their way to school, while the usual stoppages seemed to just vanish. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find techno parties in abandoned factories, not just for dancing but also for art installations that explode on weekends.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their reaction to a BART delay—actual frustration, not just a sigh. It's the difference between a missed meeting and a life interruption.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Witnessed a guy juggling for tips at the Van Ness stop while a cat in a backpack stared him down. Classic San Francisco moment right there.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all love vermut at any hour. But locals know the real vibe happens after 5 p.m., with a simple canya and some olives.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their enthusiasm for speaking Catalan, even when everyone else is comfortably slipping into Spanish.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think people queue for the bus here. It's more of a free-for-all, like a rugby match at peak hours.
I once saw a guy serenading the night bus driver with his guitar. Driver pretended to be unimpressed but was definitely tapping his foot.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a couple arguing over the best Döner kebab while a street musician performed ‘Despacito’ on his ukulele. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a Späti with a full craft beer selection, open all night, right next to a 24-hour Döner joint. Perfect combo!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell locals by their calm when BART shuts down; tourists are the ones standing in the rain with their phones out, bewildered.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy on the 19 Polk humming the mission impossible theme while keeping perfect time with the bumps. Only in SF do ninjas ride Muni.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the 'Gràcia vibe' is all about the bars. They miss the quiet corners with locals tending to their small gardens; it's a whole different world.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can usually tell by their reluctance to try a vermut at the local bar; they stick to 'cue to the next place' like tourists.
